g.data-narration: Welcome to learning about The Nature of Software, a fundamental topic in Software Engineering.
Software is a collection of programs, data, and documentation that instructs computers to perform specific tasks.
g.text: Software is a collection of programs, data, and documentation that
instructs a computer system on how to perform specific tasks.
It serves as the interface between users and hardware.

g.data-narration: Software powers everything from smartphones to spacecraft. Understanding its nature is essential for computer science professionals.
g.text: Software powers everything: from smartphones to spacecraft control
systems. Understanding software nature is essential for CS professionals.

g.data-narration: Software has five key characteristics: developed not manufactured, does not wear out, custom-built, increases with complexity, and is highly flexible.
Unlike hardware, software cannot be inspected for bugs and must be debugged. It is an intellectual product where copying is perfect.
Software types include System Software (OS, drivers), Application Software (office, browsers), and specialized categories like Embedded and AI software.
The Software Crisis of the 1960s occurred when demand for complex software exceeded the industry's ability to produce it reliably.
g.text: When demand for complex software exceeded the industry ability to produce it reliably.

g.data-narration: Software development evolved through eras: Early Computing, Structured Programming, Object-Oriented, Agile, and modern DevOps approaches.
Key software quality attributes include Correctness, Reliability, Efficiency, Usability, Maintainability, Portability, Reusability, and Security.
Practice classifying software: System software manages hardware (Windows, Android), while Application software serves user needs (MS Word, Chrome).
In summary: Software has five unique characteristics, evolved through computing eras, and quality depends on multiple attributes.
g.text: 5 Characteristics: Developed not manufactured, doesn't wear out,
Software Crisis led to Software Engineering discipline
Evolution: Early to Structured to OO to Agile to DevOps
